rule: The product should perform authorization checks when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.
The product should properly neutralize code syntax before using input in a dynamic evaluation call (e.g. 'eval').
The product should properly neutralize special elements in externally-influenced input before constructing commands, data structures, or records sent to downstream components.
The product should avoid using regular expressions with inefficient, exponential worst-case computational complexity that could consume excessive CPU cycles.
The product should properly neutralize user-controllable input before including it in web page output served to other users.
The web application should not use user-controlled input for redirects without validating or restricting the destination link.
The product should correctly perform authorization checks when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.
The product should impose intended restrictions on the size and number of resources allocated on behalf of an actor.
The product should properly neutralize special elements in externally-influenced input before constructing code segments to prevent modification of syntax or behavior.
The product should ensure that deserialized data from untrusted sources is valid and safe before use.
The product should not expose sensitive information to any actor that is not explicitly authorized to access it.
The product should properly neutralize special elements in externally-influenced input before constructing commands sent to downstream components.
The product should properly control which attributes, properties, or fields can be modified when receiving input that specifies multiple attributes to update an object.
The product should verify cryptographic signatures for data correctly.
The web server should ensure that URL requests are sent only to expected and trusted destinations.
The web application should sufficiently verify that each request was intentionally provided by the legitimate user who sent it.
The product should not use a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm or protocol.
